response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable polyglot engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js track record, but he is a poor match for this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, SQL Server or Oracle usage, or hands-on fixed income / capital markets domain knowledge. His one fintech-adjacent role at RIDE Capital involved Node.js and GraphQL rather than C++ or financial instruments, and does not satisfy either domain requirement.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would translate to disciplined delivery in any engineering environment
RIDE Capital role provides thin but non-zero fintech/investment-platform context, offering the closest domain adjacency on the resume
Solid DevOps and tooling experience (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory) aligns with some optional desirable skills around CI/CD pipelines
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at NI LabWindows plus a thesis project, versus the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL, which does not satisfy the mandatory SQL requirement for this role
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge is evidenced anywhere in the resume

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge
Strong engineering practices and CI/CD experience
